mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid spike in the unemployment rate exceeding a 0.3 percentage point monthly increase signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off equity repricing and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or monthly unemployment spikes consistently fail to predict weekly equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent weakness in residential housing starts and consumer credit outstanding indicates a synchronized demand shock that suppresses corporate revenue growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS fails to produce negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year backtest, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained industrial production growth above trend, supply chain normalization accelerates margin expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO exceeds its 12-month moving average, or rolling 3-year predictive R-squared drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in real GDP signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate profit margins, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with quarterly GDP growth revisions over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.10 percent, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when FEDFUNDS breaches the 0.10 percent threshold, or threshold-crossing events fail to predict downward price momentum in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in nonfarm payrolls growth and industrial production expansion indicates a synchronized labor-supply alignment that boosts corporate capacity utilization and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint strength in PAYEMS and INDPRO fails to predict positive quarterly equity returns in more than 40% of historical episodes, or the interaction term loses statistical significance at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation readings that stabilize near the central bank target reduce uncertainty premium in equity pricing, allowing forward-looking valuation multiples to expand and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Stabilized CPI readings consistently fail to coincide with equity multiple expansion, or forward returns remain negative when inflation volatility drops below historical median thresholds.
